args.command:
# Just perform a simple search and inspect the output
curl -s -L "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | head -n 200

# Just perform a simple search and inspect the output
curl -s -L "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | head -n 200

arguments.command:
# Just perform a simple search and inspect the output
curl -s -L "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | head -n 200

content.text: Search the web to tell who is currently the Prime Minister of Canada. Only search for one result. Give only the name with no explanation.

arguments.command: curl -s "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | grep -oP '(?<=<div class="BNeawe deIvCb AP7Wnd">)[^<]+' | head -n 1
curl -s "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | sed 's/<[^>]*>/ /g' | grep -i "Justin Trudeau" | head -n 1
curl -s "https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Canada" | grep -oP '(?<=Justin Trudeau)' | head -n 1
curl -s "https://pm.gc.ca/en" | grep -i "Prime Minister" | head -n 5
curl -s -L "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | grep -A 1 "Prime Minister of Canada" | grep -oP '(?<=<div class="BNeawe s3v9rd AP7Wnd">)[^<]+' | head -n 1

# Just perform a simple search and inspect the output
curl -s -L "https://www.google.com/search?q=current+Prime+Minister+of+Canada" | head -n 200
